about.title: About Ponpon
about.description:
Ponpon (Chen Peng-Hsuan) hails from Taipei, Taiwan, and traveled to the US to study jazz. In her musical world, instruments and the human voice blend seamlessly without clear boundaries. With her infectious vocals as a foundation, she integrates the spirit of jazz improvisation with diverse musical elements, roaming freely through melodies.

Beyond her captivating charm of musical storytelling, what fascinates fans most is her ability to simultaneously utilize crystal-clear whistling, dynamic scat singing, and guitar playing during live performances. This intertwined, multi-role performance style makes every note a carrier of emotions and stories, and has gradually become her most recognizable musical signature.

Currently, Ponpon is continuing to expand her musical territory and preparing her personal musical works, showcasing a delicate yet energetic style of creation and singing.

mentors.title: Music Mentors
mentors.description: Ponpon's musical journey has been shaped not only by her hard work but also by the guidance of many benefactors. These mentors are introduced based on Ponpon's own words, interviews, and social media posts. Below are some of the key figures she frequently mentions, though many other unlisted individuals have also supported her along the way.

list.name: George Benson

intro.bio: George Benson is a legendary American jazz guitarist and singer, and a ten-time Grammy Award winner. He is renowned for his masterful guitar skills and his signature style of flawlessly synchronizing his guitar playing with his scat singing. This unique and captivating performance style profoundly moved Ponpon and became the ultimate inspiration for her pursuit of jazz music.

list.shortDesc: In 2021, a YouTube video of his performance deeply shocked her, leading her to take a leave from school to study jazz guitar in the US. In 2025, a miraculous encounter in Phoenix allowed her to meet her idol and receive a lifelong piece of advice...

detailedStory.content: During the pandemic school closures, she accidentally stumbled upon a video of the master improvising scat while playing guitar. This revelation became the turning point that made her pause her studies and head to the US to specialize in jazz.
Bought the most expensive ticket she could afford to watch the master perform at the Hollywood Bowl. Even sitting far away watching the big screen, it felt like a dream come true.

Learning about his workshop, she drove 6 hours alone to attend. While practicing in a corner, she coincidentally met the master's musical director, Randy Waldman. Through Randy, she was granted a rare chance to watch the rehearsal from the front row. Reflecting on her difficult journey in the US, she broke down in tears of emotion for an hour.

After the show, she was invited backstage and personally thanked him. [HOVER_IMG:Benson held her hand and encouraged: "You can like my music, but please don't copy me. You've got to find your own voice."|/benson-1.jpg|https://www.facebook.com/photo/?fbid=1407946237188972&set=pcb.1407946257188970] Before checking out of her hotel, she ran into them again. The team exchanged contacts and promised to listen to her original music in the future.

Ponpon performed an original guitar solo combining whistling and humming on ABC News. When the master saw the video, he was amazed and immediately recognized the spirit passed down from him: "That's my stuff."

More than a year after their first meeting, Ponpon fulfilled her promise, [HOVER_IMG:personally delivering the master copy of her completed debut album into George Benson's hands|/benson-2.jpg|https://www.facebook.com/photo?fbid=1819123906071201&set=a.482592819724323]. They sat down for a deep conversation, and he earnestly listened to her album concepts and gave sincere advice. This exchange made her deeply feel the warmth, humility, and grace of a true master.

list.name: Lin Cheng-Ju (Teacher Geddy)

intro.bio: Founder of 'Riverside Live House,' one of Taiwan's most representative independent music performance brands, and an honors graduate of the G.I.T. program at M.I. (Musicians Institute) in the US. He is also the author of the renowned guitar method book series 'Conquering the Sea of Guitars' and has frequently served as a judge for the Golden Melody Awards and Golden Indie Music Awards. Dedicated to music education, performance, and talent cultivation for many years, he has had a profound impact on the development of jazz and independent music in Taiwan. To Ponpon, Teacher Geddy played the role of an important mentor in her jazz music learning journey, helping her establish a solid foundation in guitar and music theory, and inspiring her deeper exploration of jazz music.

list.shortDesc: Guided her into the jazz world from scratch, built a solid guitar foundation, and encouraged her to study in the US...

detailedStory.date: Supporting Her Decision to Suspend Studies
detailedStory.content: When Ponpon decided to suspend her studies to practice guitar, he highly appreciated this "burning the boats" determination, believing this focus and perseverance would be key to establishing herself in the music industry in the future.

He spent a year personally teaching her from the very basics of holding a pick and changing strings, helping her—then a "guitar novice"—build solid music theory concepts and guiding her first steps into Jazz harmony.

detailedStory.date: Lending a Precious Guitar in Support
detailedStory.content: To fully support Ponpon's learning and growth on the guitar, he generously lent her his precious Gibson ES-175 jazz guitar for practice, showing immense high regard and expectations for his beloved student.

Before Ponpon was even clear on her idol George Benson's genre positioning, Teacher Geddy recommended she listen to a large amount of Jazz and Fusion music, greatly expanding her musical horizons and becoming vital nourishment for her later focus on studying jazz in the US.

detailedStory.date: Encouraging Further Studies in the US
detailedStory.content: Discovering her passion and talent for the guitar during their lessons, he actively encouraged her to pursue further studies in California, USA, to personally explore and find a musical style that truly belonged to her.

list.name: Teacher Yun-Hui Hung

intro.bio: A veteran crossover musician, arranger, performer, and music educator in Taiwan, she studied at the Berklee College of Music in the US. Her classic 'Four-Hands Piano' series with Kay Huang is widely loved by music fans, and she won the 39th Golden Bell Award in the technical category for the Da Ai TV drama 'Quartet' soundtrack. Active on pop music and concert stages for many years, she has participated in large-scale concert tours for famous singers like Jay Chou and Stefanie Sun. To Ponpon, Teacher Hung is not only an important mentor who initiated her into piano but also provided crucial guidance and support throughout her musical learning and studies abroad.

classicSongs.name: Performing as Keyboardist at Ponpon's Solo Concert - The Price of Love

list.shortDesc: The mentor who first planted the concept of "jazz" in her mind and devised a practical strategy before she impulsively studied abroad...

detailedStory.content: To Ponpon, Teacher Yun-Hui Hung is not only an important mentor for her piano awakening but also a crucial guide on her path to jazz music. When she was 15 and learning piano chords, Teacher Hung used the jazz standard 'Blue Monk' as teaching material, allowing jazz to enter her musical world for the first time.

When Ponpon was planning to study in the US, Teacher Hung provided advice from the perspectives of language proficiency, learning efficiency, and long-term development, helping her establish a more comprehensive learning plan.

Consolidating her English, music theory, and performance basics before officially going to the US allowed her to truly invest her study abroad resources into stylistic exploration and professional refinement, laying an important foundation for her subsequent jazz music development.

list.name: Teachers Hao Ku & Tony Yang

intro.bio: Hao Ku and Tony Yang are veteran musicians and professional vocal coaches in Taiwan. With years of dedication to vocal education and the Mandarin pop music industry, they have cultivated numerous outstanding musical talents. To Ponpon, they are not only mentors who imparted singing and performance skills, but also crucial life guides who accompanied her through adolescence, guided her exploration of the musical world, and provided comprehensive support.

classicSongs.name: Ponpon Chen & Hao Ku | Live Scatting Improvisation @ Riverside Live House

list.shortDesc: Vocal mentors since she was 15, key figures who sparked her love for jazz, charity work, and her journey to study in the US...

detailedStory.content: Serving as Ponpon's vocal coaches since she was 15, they not only laid a solid foundation for her singing and performance skills but also played a pivotal role in inspiring her jazz journey and guiding her study abroad in the US.

During her exploratory phase at age 18, searching for her creative and musical direction, her mentors took her to a jazz bar in Taipei to experience live performances. They told her, "Music is more than just Mandopop." That night's eye-opening experience was her first true taste of the powerful charm of live jazz, becoming a crucial turning point that made her fall in love with and actively study the genre.

Beyond teaching vocal techniques, they led her to give back to society through music. During college, she often assisted them in charity performances at various hospitals. This experience deeply influenced her; she later formed a group with friends to sing for children at orphanages, showcasing her kind heart in using music to heal others.

When Ponpon considered suspending her studies to go to the US, her mentors gave highly practical advice, reminding her of the significant expenses and the need for clear goals to avoid wasting time. Because they knew her parents well, they actively provided guidance and engaged in months of rational discussions with her family, ultimately helping her convince them and smoothly embark on her jazz dream journey in the US.

list.name: Graham Dechter

intro.bio: Graham Dechter is a top-tier American jazz guitarist. As Ponpon's guitar teacher in the US, during his lessons, he not only imparted profound guitar techniques but also became the driving force and producer behind her debut solo jazz album.

list.shortDesc: Her guitar teacher in the US, who stepped up to produce her debut album and secured top-tier musicians for her recording...

detailedStory.date: A Crucial Career Awakening: The Need for Original Work
detailedStory.content: Having taught Ponpon guitar, he was well aware of the uncertainties and struggles of foreigners fighting for a work visa in the US (he even wrote a recommendation letter for her). He took the initiative to tell Ponpon that to truly establish a foothold in the US and start touring, she couldn't just remain a session musician without a fixed band; she "must have her own work."

detailedStory.date: Stepping Up as Producer: "I'll handle the money, consider it tuition."
detailedStory.content: When Ponpon hesitated because she didn't have a regular band and worried about the exorbitant production costs in the US, he firmly told her to "not think about the money for now" and just focus on picking the songs she was most confident in. He explained that the senior figures in the industry understood her struggles as a foreign girl in the US. Therefore, they were willing to waive their expensive production fees, treating it as "tuition" to help her get an album she could tour with.

When Ponpon was stressed about not having musicians for the recording, he pointed directly at his own stage band—top-tier session musicians who are usually impossible to book—and asked Ponpon, "Do you like my band members?" He personally stepped in to handle all personnel coordination, bringing all these master veterans into the studio to accompany her.

detailedStory.date: The Strongest Stabilizer in the Studio
detailedStory.content: The challenge of this new album was immense: Ponpon had to record five songs in one day, performing a live, synchronized "one-take" recording with these senior masters without any prior rehearsal. When Ponpon was extremely nervous, Graham, as her producer and guitar teacher who completely understood her state, constantly encouraged her in the studio, helping her complete the recording smoothly and precisely.

list.name: Ponpon's Parents

intro.bio: Although Ponpon's parents do not have a professional musical background, they have always been the strongest pillar of support on her journey to pursue her dreams. From early musical enlightenment to supporting her studies overseas after rational discussions, their complete trust, practical companionship, and selfless investment of resources have made Ponpon's international jazz dream come true step by step.

list.shortDesc: From cultivating her early musical talents to bravely letting go and funding her studies in Hollywood after rational discussions...

detailedStory.date: Early Musical Enlightenment and Talent Cultivation
detailedStory.content: Ponpon's musical enlightenment came from the naturally artistic atmosphere in her family. Her father loves listening to music, and her mother loves singing. Her family has always valued cultivating her interest in music, starting her on classical piano in kindergarten. In fifth and sixth grade, influenced by talent shows, she had the idea of becoming a singer-dancer. Although her mother initially thought it might just be a child's passing interest, she later arranged for her to take dance classes, quietly igniting her passion and longing for stage performance.

detailedStory.date: Rational Communication: Testing Determination with a "Study Abroad Financial Report"
detailedStory.content: At age 18, deeply moved by a master's video, Ponpon suddenly rushed out of her room to announce to her family that she wanted to suspend her studies to learn the electric guitar. Her father initially thought she was joking and gave a pragmatic and direct response like, "You can just learn guitar online." However, he didn't outright reject her; instead, he told her to "do more homework" first. When Ponpon started planning her studies in the US, her father required her to compile a detailed "financial report" clearly listing the living and tuition expenses for a year in the US to evaluate whether this life investment was viable. This rational requirement didn't just test her determination; it also prompted her to research schools herself and learn to take responsibility for her choices.

detailedStory.date: Bravely Letting Go and Substantial Resource Investment
detailedStory.content: After Ponpon presented a complete plan and discussed it with her mentors for months, her parents were ultimately moved by her determination. Her father personally took her to a music store to pick out her first electric guitar. Although her parents don't understand English, had never been to the US, and were full of worries about the local safety and cultural environment, they still chose to bravely let go and fully support Ponpon—who had no relatives or friends to rely on—to go to Hollywood alone to further her studies. In interviews, Ponpon has specially thanked her family, expressing gratitude for their unconditional support in pursuing her dreams.

detailedStory.date: Sponsoring the Dream Trip, Leading to a Miraculous Encounter with the Master
detailedStory.content: When Ponpon learned that her idol George Benson was holding a workshop in Phoenix, about a six-hour drive from Los Angeles, she immediately called back to Taiwan, hoping to convince her parents to support this rare dream-chasing trip. She told them it might be the only chance in her life to see her idol and learn from him up close. Her parents chose to believe in her once again and fully sponsored the trip. This investment born out of love and trust ultimately allowed Ponpon to miraculously enter the rehearsal room and interact closely with George Benson. Even the radio host couldn't help but laugh after hearing this story, saying, "Mr. and Mrs. Chen, this money was totally worth it!"

questions.question: Who lent Ponpon her Gibson ES-175 electric guitar?
